assumptions: A PowerShell environment is available and accessible.
The user has appropriate permissions to create and execute files in the target root directory.
steps: Navigate to the root directory.
Create a new file named 'hello_world.ps1'.
Add the line 'Write-Host 'Hello World'' to 'hello_world.ps1'.
Add the line 'Read-Host 'Press Enter to close...'' to 'hello_world.ps1'.
Save the 'hello_world.ps1' file.
Execute the 'hello_world.ps1' script from a PowerShell console to verify its functionality.
risks: Incorrect file path or name causing the script to be created in the wrong location or with the wrong name.

plan.first_action: Navigate to the designated root directory.

implementation_plan.technical_goal: Create a `hello_world.ps1` PowerShell script in the root directory that outputs "Hello World" and pauses for user input before exiting.
implementation_plan.architecture_impact: Minimal. This involves creating a single script file on the local file system. No changes to system configuration, network topology, or existing software architecture are anticipated.
implementation_steps: Identify and navigate to the designated root directory where the script will reside.
Create a new file named `hello_world.ps1` in the identified root directory.
Open `hello_world.ps1` for editing using a text editor or PowerShell cmdlets (e.g., `Set-Content`).

Add the following lines of PowerShell code to `hello_world.ps1`:
```powershell
Write-Host 'Hello World'
Read-Host 'Press Enter to close...'
```
Save the `hello_world.ps1` file.
Open a PowerShell console.
Navigate to the root directory containing `hello_world.ps1` within the PowerShell console.
Execute the script by typing `.\hello_world.ps1` and pressing Enter.
validation_criteria: The file `hello_world.ps1` must exist in the specified root directory.
The content of `hello_world.ps1` must exactly contain `Write-Host 'Hello World'` followed by `Read-Host 'Press Enter to close...'`, ensuring proper syntax and line breaks.
When `hello_world.ps1` is executed from a PowerShell console, it must display the text "Hello World" on the console.
After displaying "Hello World", the script must pause execution and display a prompt (e.g., "Press Enter to close...") until the user presses the Enter key.
The script must terminate successfully after the user presses Enter.
